{"subagent_type":"Explore","description":"Explore Next.js project structure","prompt":"I need to understand a Next.js project that uses Pages Router. Please thoroughly explore the workspace at /home/user/workspace and give me:

1. The full directory tree (top 4-5 levels)
2. The contents of package.json (dependencies, especially Next.js version)
3. The contents of tsconfig.json (if it exists)
4. All files inside the `pages/` directory
5. All files inside the `public/` directory
6. List all layout/component files outside pages/ (like components/, layouts/, etc.)
7. The contents of next.config.js or next.config.mjs
8. Any middleware.ts files
9. Look for any `_app.tsx`, `_document.tsx` files
10. Check for api/ routes
11. Look at the first 50 lines of each page file to understand the patterns used

Be very thorough - list every file in detail with its sizes."}

<output>
Here is the complete and thorough analysis of the Next.js project at `/home/user/workspace`.

---

## 1. Full Directory Tree (Top 5 Levels)

```
/home/user/workspace/
├── .gitignore                                    (412 B)
├── components/
│   └── AppProvider.js                            (495 B)
├── next.config.ts                                (104 B)
├── package.json                                  (602 B)
├── package-lock.json                             (151 KB)
├── pages/
│   ├── 404.js                                    (588 B)
│   ├── _app.js                                   (602 B)
│   ├── _document.js                              (584 B)
│   ├── _error.js                                 (714 B)
│   ├── index.js                                  (1.3 KB)
│   ├── api/
│   │   └── posts/
│   │       ├── [id].js                           (984 B)
│   │       └── index.js                          (837 B)
│   └── blog/
│       ├── [id].js                               (1.9 KB)
│       └── index.js                              (1.1 KB)
├── styles/
│   └── globals.css                               (1.4 KB)
└── tsconfig.json                                 (667 B)
```

The project has no `public/` directory, no `layouts/` directory, no `lib/`, no `middleware.ts`, and no `next-env.d.ts` (it is gitignored).

---

## 2. package.json -- Dependencies

```json
{
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "next build",
    "start": "next start"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@ai-sdk/react": "2.0.0-beta.28",
    "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "^1.17.0",
    "ai": "5.0.59",
    "next": "16.2.0-canary.41",
    "react": "19.1.0",
    "react-dom": "19.1.0",
    "zod": "^4.0.10"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^20",
    "@types/react": "^19",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19",
    "typescript": "^5",
    "vitest": "^3.1.3",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.4.1",
    "vite-tsconfig-paths": "^5.1.4"
  }
}
```

**Key takeaways:**
- Next.js version: **16.2.0-canary.41** (a canary build, not a stable release)
- React version: **19.1.0**
- Uses ESM (`"type": "module"`)
- Notable extra dependencies: `ai` SDK v5, `@ai-sdk/react`, `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, `zod` v4
- Testing: `vitest` v3 with `@vitejs/plugin-react`

---

## 3. tsconfig.json

File: `/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json` (667 B)

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2017",
    "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"],
    "allowJs": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "module": "esnext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "incremental": true,
    "plugins": [{"name": "next"}],
    "paths": {"@/*": ["./*"]}
  },
  "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", ".next/types/**/*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}
```

Notable: `"allowJs": true` is set because most source files are plain `.js`, not `.ts`/`.tsx`. The `@/*` path alias maps to the project root (`./*`).

---

## 4. All Files in `pages/`

### pages/_app.js (602 B)
The custom App component. Wraps all pages with:
- `AppProvider` (from `../components/AppProvider`) -- a React Context providing theme state
- A layout shell: `<header>` with site title "My Blog" and nav links to Home and Blog, a `<main>` wrapper, and a `<footer>` with copyright
- Global CSS import

### pages/_document.js (584 B)
Custom Document. Sets:
- `<Html lang="en">`
- Meta description, favicon link, Google Fonts preconnect and Inter font loading
- Standard `<Main />` / `<NextScript />` body structure

### pages/_error.js (714 B)
Custom error page using `getInitialProps`. Handles both server-side errors (from `res.statusCode`) and client-side errors (from `err.statusCode`). …(+7731)
